message:
perf(asset-state): add inspect_semantic flag to skip semantic DB open on fast paths

inspect_search_assets() unconditionally called inspect_semantic_assets(),
which opens the embeddings DB and walks the model directory. On the
state_meta_json fast paths that only need lexical readiness, that work
was wasted — but couldn't be avoided cleanly because the function had no
way to express "skip semantic inspection."

Add `inspect_semantic: bool` to InspectSearchAssetsInput. When false,
inspect_search_assets() routes to the new helper
semantic_state_not_inspected(), which returns a SemanticAssetState
populated with cheap-to-compute fields only:

- status / availability = "not_inspected"
- preferred_backend resolved from the SemanticPreference enum
- model_dir resolved via FastEmbedder::default_model_dir() when the
  preference is DefaultModel (no DB open, no model walk)
- progressive_ready computed from
  semantic_progressive_assets_ready(data_dir) (cheap stat checks)
- fast_tier / quality_tier / backlog / checkpoint computed from
  semantic_manifest_progress(), which reads the manifest file only —
  no DB open
- hint pointing operators at `cass status --json` or
  `cass models status --json` if they want the full readiness picture

Wire the flag from state_meta_json_inner() in src/lib.rs:
inspect_semantic = allow_db_open && !skip_db_open. The two existing
booleans already gate every other "may we touch the DB" decision in
state_meta_json, so the semantic inspection now follows the same rule.

Existing tests are updated to pass `inspect_semantic: true` (no
behavior change). New test
inspect_search_assets_can_skip_semantic_db_open_for_fast_paths
verifies that with `inspect_semantic: false`:
- the DB path is intentionally an unopenable directory, yet
  inspection succeeds
- semantic.status / semantic.availability are "not_inspected"
- semantic.fallback_mode is Some("lexical")
- lexical inspection still runs and does not return "error"
